title: Heap — Last BFS Descendant
description:
A max-heap is stored in a 1-indexed array where t[0] = n (size). Given node index i, find the index of its last descendant in BFS order (the last node among all descendants when scanning level by level). If i has no descendants, return i itself.

Recall: left child of k is 2k, right child is 2k+1.
inputSpec: First line: n (heap size, 1-indexed). Second line: n integers (the heap values). Third line: i (1-indexed node).
outputSpec: The index of the last BFS descendant of i (or i itself if it's a leaf).

botCodes.code:
n=int(input())
a=list(map(int,input().split()))
i=int(input())
g=i;d=i
while 2*g<=n:
    g=2*g
    d=2*d+1
print(min(d,n))

#include<stdio.h>
int main(){int n;scanf("%d",&n);for(int j=0;j<n;j++){int x;scanf("%d",&x);}int i;scanf("%d",&i);int g=i,d=i;while(2*g<=n){g=2*g;d=2*d+1;}printf("%d\n",d<n?d:n);}

let()=let n=Scanf.scanf" %d" Fun.id in for _=1 to n do ignore(Scanf.scanf" %d" Fun.id) done;let i=Scanf.scanf" %d" Fun.id in let g=ref i and d=ref i in while 2* !g<=n do g:=2* !g;d:=2* !d+1 done;Printf.printf"%d\n"(min !d n)
